intro: Chamaecrista glaucofilix, also known as the golden button or golden senna, is a plant species belonging to the legume family, characterized by its yellow flowers and small, button-like seed pods. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Chamaecrista glaucofilix include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Chamaecrista glaucofilix include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation and alleviate respiratory issues.
